MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-20-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF LIN WANG'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ORDER STRIKING PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED ON ALL GROUNDS LISTED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND/OR POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. RE: EXTRINSIC FRAUD/MISTAKE: NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO STRIKE WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WAS SERVED ELECTRONICALLY ON APRIL 24, 2015. FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IF ANY WAS DUE BY MAY 6, 2015. NO EVIDENCE IS PRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT PLAINTIFF'S FORMER COUNSEL AT THAT TIME (OR AT THE TIME OF HEARING ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE) EXPERIENCED ANY MEDICAL/OR OTHER PROBLEMS, WHICH COULD POTENTIALLY CONSTITUTE EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE. RE: RELIEF PER CCP 473(B): THIS MOTION IS UNTIMELY. RE: RELIEF PER CCP 128: NO SUCH RELIEF IS SOUGHT PER THE NOTICE OF MOTION. PER THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES RELIEF SOUGHT PURSUANT TO THIS SECTION IS "TO SUBSTITUTE PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY IMMEDIATELY." THERE IS NO INDICATION THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE SEEKING SUBSTITUTION OF THEIR CURRENT COUNSEL, ALEX LEMIEUX. RE: LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUCH RELIEF IS NOT SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. NO PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT ATTACHED TO THE MOTION AND NO DECLARATION PER CRC 3.1324 FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MING XUE'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTIONS 2023.010 AND 2030.290 (C) IS GRANTED. RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, SHALL BE PROVIDED TO THE PROPOUNDING PARTY WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. SANCTIONS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,200 TO BE PAID WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE OPPOSITION WAS FILED 2 DAYS LATE AND NO COURTESY COPIES OF OPPOSITION WERE PROVIDED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADDRESS IN GOOD FAITH ALL ISSUES RAISED IN THIS MOTION TO STRIKE. PAGE 3, PARAGRAPH 10(C); PAGE 3, PARAGRAPH 10(E); PAGE 3, FROM PARAGRAPH 10(F) THE FOLLOWING: "WRONGFUL EVICTION; ASSAULT; BATTERY; FALSE ARREST & IMPRISONMENT; FRAUD; NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION; INTENTIONAL & NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; DECLARATORY RELIEF; INJUNCTIVE RELIEF; CONSPIRACY; VIOLATION OF CIVIL RIGHTS; ABUSE OF PROCESS; MALICIOUS PROSECUTION; TRESPAS [SIC] TO PROPERTY, DEFAMATION, LIBEL, SLANDER AND CONVERSION"; PAGE 3, PARAGRAPH 11(G); PAGE 3, PARAGRAPH 14(A)(2); PAGE 8, EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ATTACHMENT ARE STRICKEN FROM THE COMPLAINT AS THEY ARE NOT DRAWN OR FILED IN CONFORMITY A COURT RULE AND ARE IRRELEVANT, FALSE, OR IMPROPER. (CODE CIV. PROC., SECTION 436.). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Order Striking Portions Of The Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LIN WANG's Motion For Relief From Order Striking Portions Of The Complaint is DENIED on all grounds listed in the Notice of Motion and/or Points and Authorities.
Re: Extrinsic fraud/mistake: Notice of Entry of Order Granting Motion to Strike with leave to amend was served electronically on April 24, 2015. First Amended Complaint, if any was due by May 6, 2015. No evidence is presented to the Court that Plaintiff's former counsel at that time (or at the time of hearing on the Motion to Strike) experienced any medical/or other problems, which could potentially constitute extrinsic fraud or mistake. Re: relief per CCP 473(b): This motion is untimely. Re: relief per CCP 128: No such relief is sought per the Notice of Motion. Per the Points and Authorities relief sought pursuant to this section is "to substitute Plaintiff's attorney immediately." There is no indication that Plaintiffs are seeking substitution of their current counsel, Alex Lemieux. Re: Leave to File Amended Complaint: such relief is not sought in the Notice of Motion. No proposed amended complaint attached to the motion and no declaration per CRC 3.1324 filed. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D)
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alex Lemieux, Esq., (415-664-4280) for Plaintiff/Opposing party Lin Wang and Fen Ying Yin.
Bryan Stofferahn, Esq., (415-434-3006) of Connor, Bishop & Stofferahn, LLP for Defendant/Moving party Ming Xue.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT MING XUE's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2023.030(D) is Denied both proceedurally and substantially. The Court rules that no money was due at the time motion was filed. The further rules that the Plaintiff still owes $2,200.00 the Defendant in sanctions. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Continued To Nov-10-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 2023.010 And 2030.290 (C)
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Bryan Stofferahn, Esq. (415-434-3006)of Connor, Bishop & Stofferahn, LLP for Defendant/Opposing party Ming Xue.
Alex Lemieux Esq. (805-3541-8784) for Plaintiff/Moving party Lin Wang and Fen Ying Yin.

Substituiion of attorney E-filed by Alex Lemieux is pending as of today's hearing date. Alex Lenieux did not comply with TR 3.1308. Plaintiff's request for sanctions not be granted is DENIED.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT MING XUE's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 2023.010 And 2030.290 (C) is GRANTED. Responses to Form Interrogatories, without objections, shall be provided to the propounding party within 30 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,200 to be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of order. The Court notes that the opposition was filed 2 days late and no courtesy copies of opposition were provided. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: Defendant's Motion to Strike Portions of Complaint is GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to address in good faith all issues raised in this Motion to Strike. Page 3, paragraph 10(c); page 3, paragraph 10(e); page 3, from paragraph 10(f) the following: "Wrongful Eviction; Assault; Battery; False Arrest & Imprisonment; Fraud; Negligent Misrepresentation; Intentional & Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress; Declaratory Relief; Injunctive relief; Conspiracy; Violation of Civil rights; Abuse of Process; Malicious Prosecution; Trespas [sic] to Property, Defamation, Libel, Slander and Conversion"; page 3, paragraph 11(g); page 3, paragraph 14(a)(2); page 8, Exemplary Damages Attachment are stricken from the Complaint as they are not drawn or filed in conformity a court rule and are irrelevant, false, or improper. (Code Civ. Proc., Section 436.). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
